title: Unexpected modify column cause data-index inconsistency
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

``` SQL
CREATE TABLE `t` (
  `col6bf3` double NOT NULL DEFAULT '1.7493807071127492',
  `col7044` varchar(145) CHARACTER SET utf8 COLLATE utf8_general_ci DEFAULT 'xdwzi7tpmjwxeklahyxvg3gl0mt2x08mdhxz9pbdsdnrvj8qghrckfzat38jkukpycmtwsl3n8nnw9m5hgavfeu8nnhf8e5c7yttr8',
  `col397e` date DEFAULT '6275-04-28',
  `colecb5` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `col2611` float DEFAULT '1.6870946',
  PRIMARY KEY (`col6bf3`) /*T![clustered_index] CLUSTERED */
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin COMMENT='4a6d1ec3'

```

DO DDL concurrently:
``` 
DDL1:
ALTER TABLE t ADD INDEX idx (col7044)

DDL2:
ALTER TABLE `t` modify column `col7044` VARCHAR(206) NULL DEFAULT 'emtlqampngugbkxqy' FIRST
```

Make DDL1 execute first.

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

The second DDL should fail
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
Both of the DDLs are successful.
### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
master
<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table ( id INTEGER NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, value DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (id) );
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test1', 10.50), ('test2', NULL), ('test3', 25.75);
ALTER TABLE test_table ALTER COLUMN value SET DATA TYPE DECIMAL(12,4);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR value > 20.00;
key_bug_features: Concurrent DDL modifies column while adding index
